column2: Following the collapse of the Revolutionary Roman Republic's hold over the continent, many of the reconstituted monarchies have struggled to maintain what they previously held. Portugal is no exception. The wars and political tumult have left the once sprawling colonial empire in pieces. Though the Portuguese retain fragments of their colonies, their oversees footholds may not be long for this world. If Portugal is to reclaim its former glory, it must delicately balance its interests in restoring its colonies with the growing threat of continental neighbors that smell blood in the water.
